$comment: agent-ready-guardrails canonical permission policy. Installed into ~/.claude/settings.json (or /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json) by scripts/install-policy.sh. RULE OF THUMB: a user-level deny can NEVER be overridden by a project-level allow, so deny is reserved for the catastrophic; anything legitimate-locally-but-dangerous-in-prod goes to ask. This is a codebase-agnostic BASELINE. Extend it per your stack (see README.md 'Extending the baseline'): add schema-migration/deploy commands to `ask`; add your providers' token prefixes (sntrys_, xoxb-, ATATT, ...) and any extra credential stores (~/.config/<org>/) to `deny`; put a repo's own prod-host fences in that repo's .claude/settings.json.
